title: The veil of ignorance
intro: You are in a room with no mirror. The cloth takes your name, your face, your health.
You do not know whether you will wake rich or starving, gifted or slow, loved or owed nothing.
Blind to all of it, you are handed the pen. You write the rules everyone lives by, including the one you become.

rounds.title: The harvest
rounds.question: The year's whole work sits in one pile. How is it divided?

voices.count: Every plate the same. The strongest hands stop trying past what feeds them.
voices.faith: No one bows to receive bread. That holds for everyone here.
voices.tend: The sick child gets a full bowl tonight.
voices.agree: Not knowing whose hands you wake with, would you want the share fixed?
voices.virtue: You make a people who sit at one table. Effort stops meaning much there.

choices.label: The most to those with least.

voices.count: The pile is the same size. You just moved where it lands.
voices.faith: Need is a claim, not a favour. You wrote it as a claim.
voices.tend: The thinnest arm gets the heaviest sack.
voices.agree: Not knowing whose body you wake in, would you want need to come first?
voices.virtue: You make a people who look down the table first. The able wait.

voices.count: Output rises. So does the gap between the top plate and the bottom.
voices.faith: What a person makes is theirs. You did not take it from them.
voices.tend: The woman with the cough keeps what the cough let her earn.
voices.agree: Not knowing how strong you wake, would you want the harvest to follow the hands?
voices.virtue: You make a people who keep their own. Some of them keep very little.

rounds.title: The floor
rounds.question: Somewhere a person is falling. Set the law for how far down the fall may go.

choices.label: Guarantee a floor no one falls below.

voices.count: The average drops. The bottom stops dropping.
voices.faith: No one may be left on the ground. You wrote the floor in.
voices.agree: Not knowing how far you fall, would you want something under you?
voices.virtue: You make a people who catch. Catching costs the ones who never fall.

choices.label: Lift the average as high as it goes.

voices.count: The average is the highest of the three. Somebody is under it.
voices.faith: A person is not a rung for the total to climb. You made some into rungs.
voices.tend: The ones who fell fell all the way.
voices.agree: Not knowing where you land, would you want the total to decide?
voices.virtue: You make a people who climb well. You do not ask who they stood on.

voices.count: Both ends run free. The spread is the widest you can write.
voices.faith: Every hand is left to its own work. Nobody is owed a rescue.
voices.tend: There is no one at the bottom of the stairs.
voices.agree: Not knowing whose luck you draw, would you want no one waiting below?
voices.virtue: You make a people who stand alone. Some of them stand very badly.

rounds.title: The stranger
rounds.question: A stranger arrives at dusk with empty hands and no claim on anyone. What are they owed?

choices.label: A full share, as one of us.

voices.count: One more plate at the same table. Every share gets slightly thinner.
voices.faith: A person at the door is a person. You counted them as one.
voices.tend: They ate before they explained themselves.
voices.agree: Not knowing which side of the door you wake on, would you want it open?
voices.virtue: You make a people who widen the table. It costs everyone a little.

voices.count: Nothing now, something later. The sum is unchanged tonight.
voices.faith: A share follows the work. You did not hand out what was not made.
voices.tend: They slept with their hands still empty.
voices.agree: Not knowing whose hands arrive empty, would you want the door to wait?
voices.virtue: You make a people who ask first what you brought. Some arrive with nothing.

choices.label: Whatever they need to stand again.

voices.count: The most goes to the one who counts for least right now.
voices.faith: Need does not check a passport. You wrote it that way.
voices.tend: Someone took their coat and hung it up.
voices.agree: Not knowing whose road ends here, would you want the room to hold?
voices.virtue: You make a people who lift the newest first. The settled wait their turn.

positions.label: the weakest, sick and born with nothing
a stranger, arriving with empty hands
an ordinary life, neither high nor low
the fortunate, strong and born to plenty

equal.weakest: You wake unable to work. Your bowl is the same as everyone's — enough to live on, never an inch more, however hard the cough gets.
equal.stranger: You arrive with nothing and are handed a full share the same evening. Nobody asks what you brought.
equal.average: You wake in the middle and stay there. Your days are even. So is everyone else's.
equal.fortunate: You wake strong and quick, and your plate is the same size as the slowest hand at the table.

worstoff.weakest: You wake unable to work, and the room turns toward you first. You are carried by a rule you wrote blind.
worstoff.stranger: You arrive with nothing and the society moves to put you back on your feet. It was written before you knocked.
worstoff.average: You wake in the wide middle. You rise more slowly than you might have. You never watch the ground come up.
worstoff.fortunate: You wake strong, and a good part of what your hands make goes downward. You never notice the floor you paid for.

desert.weakest: You wake unable to work, in a world that pays hands. Yours do not close. There is nothing under you.
desert.stranger: You arrive with nothing and are told to earn it. Some nights you do not.
desert.average: You wake in the middle and keep exactly what you make. It is enough, most years.
desert.fortunate: You wake strong and keep all of it. Nobody asks you for anything. Nobody would have caught you either.

sum.weakest: You wake unable to work in a world tuned for the total. You are the part of the total that got rounded off.
sum.stranger: You arrive with nothing. The average is high here, and you are not in it yet.
sum.average: You wake in the wide middle of a world built to lift its average, and it carries you. You rarely think about the few underneath.
sum.fortunate: You wake strong in a world that rewards what climbs. It climbs on something. You are not asked to look.

weakest.tend: Someone is holding the bowl. You wrote whose hand it is.
weakest.agree: You set this rule without knowing you would be the one under it.
weakest.faith: Whatever you are owed here, you owed it to yourself first, blind.

stranger.tend: You knocked, and the door did what you told it to do.
stranger.agree: You wrote the door's rule before you knew you would be outside it.
stranger.faith: The rule does not know your face. That was the point of writing it blind.

average.tend: Nobody will write about your year. You wrote it anyway.
average.agree: Most lives land here. You were deciding for most lives without knowing it.
average.faith: The middle is where a rule gets tested quietly, every ordinary day.

fortunate.tend: You wake with more than you need. Someone else woke with the rest of it.
fortunate.agree: You wrote this not knowing you would be the one it favours.
fortunate.faith: A rule that happens to suit you is the hardest one to check.

ending.served: You wrote a floor when you could not see your own feet, and you landed on it. Whatever else the rule cost, it caught you.
ending.betrayed: You wrote a world that spends its weakest, and then you woke as one of them. Nobody broke the rule. The rule was the thing.
ending.windfall: You wrote a world that lets the strong keep what they take, and you woke strong. Nothing here was taken from you. Some of it was taken for you.
ending.sacrifice: You wrote a floor you did not need, and you woke above it. It costs you every year, quietly, and you have never once had to use it.
ending.close: The cloth is off and the pen is down. You live inside the world you drew without knowing your own face — which is the only honest way anyone draws one.
